Lagundi.name: Lagundi
Lagundi.description: Lagundi is a shrub that grows up to 5 meters tall. It has distinctive five-fingered leaves (palmate compound leaves) and small blue to lavender flowers. It is one of the most widely used medicinal plants in the Philippines, recognized by the Department of Health for its effectiveness.
preparation_steps: Boil a handful of fresh or dried leaves in 2 cups of water for 15 minutes
Let it cool and strain the liquid
Drink as tea, 1 cup 3 times daily
For cough relief, add honey to taste
Lagundi.cultural_relevance: Lagundi has been used in Philippine traditional medicine for centuries. It is one of the ten herbal medicines endorsed by the Department of Health (DOH) for its proven medicinal properties. The plant is commonly found in backyards and is often the first remedy for cough and colds in Filipino households.
Lagundi.detailed_description: Lagundi (Vitex negundo) is one of the ten medicinal plants officially endorsed by the Philippine Department of Health (DOH) for its proven effectiveness in treating respiratory ailments including cough, asthma, and pharyngitis.

Sambong.name: Sambong
Sambong.description: Sambong is a coarse erect annual herb that grows up to 1.5 meters tall. It has soft, hairy leaves that emit a camphor-like scent when crushed. The plant produces yellow flowers in clusters at the top. It thrives in open grasslands and waste places throughout the Philippines.
preparation_steps: Boil 10-15 leaves in 3 cups of water for 20 minutes
Drink 1 cup, 3 times daily after meals
Sambong.cultural_relevance: Sambong is officially recognized by the Philippine Department of Health as a medicinal plant for its diuretic properties and effectiveness in treating kidney stones. It has been used by traditional healers (albularyo) for generations and remains a popular herbal remedy in rural communities.
Sambong.detailed_description: Sambong (Blumea balsamifera) is officially recognized by the Philippine DOH for its diuretic properties and effectiveness in dissolving kidney stones and treating urinary tract infections.

Bayabas.name: Bayabas
Bayabas.description: Bayabas (Guava) is a small evergreen tree or shrub that grows up to 10 meters tall. It has smooth, oblong to elliptic leaves with prominent veins. The bark is smooth and peels off in thin flakes. It produces white flowers and yellow-green edible fruits with pink or white flesh.
preparation_steps: For diarrhea: Boil 10 fresh leaves in 2 cups of water for 10 minutes
For wound wash: Boil a handful of leaves until water turns brown
Cool and use to wash affected area 2-3 times daily
For mouthwash: Gargle with cooled boiled leaf water
Bayabas.cultural_relevance: Bayabas is ubiquitous in the Philippines, found in almost every backyard. It is one of the most important traditional medicines, especially for treating diarrhea in children. The leaves are readily available year-round, making it an accessible first-aid remedy in rural areas.
Bayabas.detailed_description: Bayabas or Guava (Psidium guajava) is endorsed by the Philippine DOH as an effective treatment for diarrhea and amebiasis. The leaves contain tannins that provide antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.

Ampalaya.name: Ampalaya
Ampalaya.description: Ampalaya is a climbing vine with stems that grow up to 5 meters long. It has long, flat, grass-like leaves and produces small white flowers. The bulb consists of 4-20 cloves enclosed in a white or pinkish papery skin. It has a pungent, characteristic odor.
preparation_steps: Wash and slice fresh leaves or fruit
Boil 1 cup of sliced leaves in 2 cups water for 15 minutes
Alternatively, juice fresh leaves and mix with water
Drink 1/2 cup, twice daily before meals
Ampalaya.cultural_relevance: Ampalaya is a staple vegetable in Filipino cuisine and medicine. The Department of Health recognizes its anti-diabetic properties. Despite its bitter taste, it is valued for its numerous health benefits and is often used in the traditional dish 'ginisang ampalaya.'
Ampalaya.detailed_description: Ampalaya or Bitter Melon (Momordica charantia) is recognized by the Philippine DOH for its anti-diabetic properties. It contains charantin and momordicin which help lower blood glucose levels.

Malunggay.name: Malunggay
Malunggay.description: Malunggay is a fast-growing, drought-resistant tree that can reach up to 10 meters in height. It has compound leaves with small oval leaflets, white fragrant flowers, and long, slender seed pods (drumsticks). Every part of the tree is edible and nutritious.
medicinal_uses: Boosts milk production for nursing mothers
Rich source of vitamins and minerals
preparation_steps: Collect fresh leaves and tender stems
Add to soups, stews, or sauté with garlic
For tea: Steep fresh or dried leaves in hot water for 5 minutes
Malunggay.cultural_relevance: Malunggay is called the 'miracle tree' and 'nature's medicine cabinet.' It is a staple in Filipino kitchens, used in tinola, ginataan, and various dishes. Its nutritional value makes it essential for combating malnutrition in rural communities.
Malunggay.detailed_description: Malunggay (Moringa oleifera) is called the 'Miracle Tree' and is endorsed by the DOH as a nutritional supplement. It contains 7x more Vitamin C than oranges and 4x more Vitamin A than carrots.

Niyog-niyogan.name: Niyog-niyogan
Niyog-niyogan.description: Niyog-niyogan is a climbing vine with reddish-brown stems that can reach up to 8 meters. It produces elliptical leaves and showy fragrant flowers that change color from white to pink to red. The fruit is oblong and turns brown when mature.
Niyog-niyogan.cultural_relevance: Niyog-niyogan is primarily known as a deworming remedy in the Philippines. The plant is often grown as an ornamental vine due to its beautiful changing-color flowers.
Niyog-niyogan.detailed_description: Niyog-niyogan (Quisqualis indica) is traditionally used as an anthelmintic to expel intestinal worms. The seeds contain quisqualic acid which is effective against Ascaris and other parasitic worms.

Pansit-pansitan.name: Pansit-pansitan
Pansit-pansitan.description: Pansit-pansitan is a small, succulent annual herb that grows 15-45 cm tall. It has light green, fleshy, translucent leaves with heart-shaped bases. It thrives in damp, shaded areas.
preparation_steps: May be eaten raw in salads
For tea: Boil 1 cup in 2 cups water
Pansit-pansitan.cultural_relevance: Pansit-pansitan is commonly found in damp corners of Filipino homes. It is valued for treating gout and arthritis and is often eaten as a salad vegetable.
Pansit-pansitan.detailed_description: Pansit-pansitan (Peperomia pellucida) is traditionally used for treating arthritis, gout, and kidney stones. It has diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties that help lower uric acid levels.

Yerba Buena.name: Yerba Buena
Yerba Buena.description: Yerba Buena is a perennial creeping herb with hairy, square stems. It has small, oval toothed leaves that emit a strong minty aroma when crushed. It produces small white or purple flowers.
Yerba Buena.cultural_relevance: Yerba Buena (good herb) has been used by Filipino families for generations as a pain reliever. Its minty aroma makes it pleasant to use.
Yerba Buena.detailed_description: Yerba Buena (Clinopodium douglasii) is one of the ten DOH-endorsed medicinal plants for pain relief. Its menthol content provides effective relief from body aches, headaches, and menstrual cramps.

Bawang.name: Bawang
Bawang.description: Bawang is a bulbous perennial plant that grows up to 1 meter tall. It has long, flat leaves and produces small white flowers. The bulb has 4-20 cloves with a pungent odor.
preparation_steps: Swallow with water or mix with honey
Bawang.cultural_relevance: Bawang is indispensable in Filipino cooking and medicine. Every Filipino kitchen has garlic, used both as spice and medicine.
Bawang.detailed_description: Bawang or Garlic (Allium sativum) is recognized by the DOH for its cardiovascular benefits. It contains allicin which helps lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol, and prevent blood clot formation.

Akapulko.name: Akapulko
Akapulko.description: Akapulko is a coarse erect shrub that grows 1-3 meters tall. It has bright yellow flowers arranged in a candelabra shape. The leaves are compound with 8-12 leaflets.
preparation_steps: For bath: Add to bath water
Akapulko.cultural_relevance: Akapulko is the go-to remedy for fungal skin infections. Its distinctive yellow flowers make it easy to identify.
Akapulko.detailed_description: Akapulko or Ringworm Bush (Senna alata) is DOH-endorsed for treating fungal skin infections. The leaves contain chrysophanic acid which is effective against ringworm, scabies, and eczema.

Tsaang Gubat.name: Tsaang Gubat
Tsaang Gubat.description: Tsaang Gubat is a shrub that grows 1-3 meters tall. It has small, oval leaves and produces small white flowers. It is commonly found in forests and wild areas.
Tsaang Gubat.cultural_relevance: Tsaang Gubat is used as a tea substitute and for treating stomach ailments in rural communities.
Tsaang Gubat.detailed_description: Tsaang Gubat (Ehretia microphylla) is endorsed by the Philippine DOH for treating diarrhea and stomach problems. It has anti-spasmodic and anti-diarrheal properties.

Alagaw.name: Alagaw
Alagaw.description: Alagaw is a shrub or small tree with fragrant leaves. It grows in forests and is used for respiratory ailments.
Alagaw.cultural_relevance: Alagaw is used in traditional medicine for respiratory problems.
Alagaw.detailed_description: Alagaw (Premna odorata) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats cough, Relieves asthma, Expectorant. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Avocado.name: Avocado
Avocado.description: Avocado is an evergreen tree that produces nutrient-dense fruits with creamy texture. The leaves are also used medicinally for kidney stones and respiratory problems.
medicinal_uses: Rich in healthy fats and nutrients
preparation_steps: For leaves tea: Collect young leaves, wash thoroughly
Boil leaves in water for 10-15 minutes
Avocado.cultural_relevance: Avocado is popular worldwide for its creamy, nutritious fruit. The leaves are traditionally used for kidney and respiratory problems.
Avocado.detailed_description: Avocado (Persea americana) is valued for its nutrient-rich fruit containing healthy fats, fiber, and vitamins. The leaves are used in traditional medicine for treating kidney stones and respiratory issues, containing beneficial flavonoids and phenolic compounds.

Basil.name: Basil
Basil.description: Basil is an aromatic herb with oval leaves. It is widely used in cooking and traditional medicine.
Basil.cultural_relevance: Balanoi is used in Filipino cooking and for treating cough and colds.
Basil.detailed_description: Basil (Ocimum basilicum) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Relieves cough, Digestive aid, Anti-stress. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Bignay.name: Bignay
Bignay.description: Bignay is a small tree with leathery leaves. It produces small red fruits that are edible and medicinal.
Bignay.cultural_relevance: Bignay fruits are eaten fresh and used in traditional medicine for fever.
Bignay.detailed_description: Bignay (Antidesma bunius) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats fever, Anti-inflammatory, Rich in antioxidants. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Calamansi.name: Calamansi
Calamansi.description: Calamansi is a small citrus fruit tree. The fruits are used for cooking and medicinal purposes.
Calamansi.cultural_relevance: Calamansi is an essential ingredient in Filipino cuisine and home remedies.
Calamansi.detailed_description: Calamansi (Citrus microcarpa) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Rich in Vitamin C, Treats cough, Bleaching agent. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Carrot.name: Carrot
Carrot.description: Carrot is a root vegetable known for its bright orange color and high Vitamin A content. It is widely cultivated and consumed worldwide.
Carrot.cultural_relevance: Carrots are popular as a healthy snack and ingredient in Filipino dishes.
Carrot.detailed_description: Carrot (Daucus carota) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Rich in Vitamin A, Good for eye health, Antioxidant properties. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Cassava.name: Cassava
Cassava.description: Cassava is a woody shrub with starchy tuberous roots. It is a major source of carbohydrates.
Cassava.cultural_relevance: Cassava is an important staple food, especially in rural areas.
Cassava.detailed_description: Cassava (Manihot esculenta) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Energy source, Gluten-free, Starchy food. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Coconut.name: Coconut
Coconut.description: Coconut is a palm tree with versatile uses. Every part of the coconut tree is useful.
Coconut.cultural_relevance: Coconut is called the 'Tree of Life' in the Philippines for its many uses.
Coconut.detailed_description: Coconut (Cocos nucifera) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Hydration, Rich in electrolytes, Antibacterial. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Cucumber.name: Cucumber
Cucumber.description: Cucumber is a widely cultivated plant in the gourd family. It has a mild, refreshing taste and high water content.
Cucumber.cultural_relevance: Cucumbers are used fresh in salads and as a natural beauty remedy.
Cucumber.detailed_description: Cucumber (Cucumis sativus) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Hydrating, Skin health, Aids digestion. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Dalandan.name: Dalandan
Dalandan.description: Dalandan is a citrus tree with sour fruits. The fruits are used for juice and traditional medicine.
Dalandan.cultural_relevance: Dalandan juice is a popular refreshment and traditional remedy.
Dalandan.detailed_description: Dalandan (Citrus aurantium) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Rich in Vitamin C, Digestive aid, Appetite stimulant. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Dayap.name: Dayap
Dayap.description: Dayap is a citrus plant with bumpy fruits and aromatic leaves. It is used in cooking and medicine.
Dayap.cultural_relevance: Dayap is valued for its aromatic properties in cooking and medicine.
Dayap.detailed_description: Dayap (Citrus hystrix) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Digestive aid, Cough remedy, Skin care. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Duhat.name: Duhat
Duhat.description: Duhat is a tree with oblong leaves and dark purple fruits. The bark and leaves are medicinal.
Duhat.cultural_relevance: Duhat fruits are eaten fresh and used traditionally for diabetes.
Duhat.detailed_description: Duhat (Syzygium cumini) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats diabetes, Anti-diarrheal, Controls blood sugar. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Eucalyptus.name: Eucalyptus
Eucalyptus.description: Eucalyptus is a tall tree with aromatic leaves. It is known for its strong medicinal scent.
Eucalyptus.cultural_relevance: Eucalyptus is used for respiratory problems and as a natural decongestant.
Eucalyptus.detailed_description: Eucalyptus (Eucalyptus globulus) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Relieves cough, Decongestant, Antiseptic. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Ginger.name: Ginger
Ginger.description: Ginger is a flowering plant with aromatic rhizomes. It is widely used in cooking and medicine.
Ginger.cultural_relevance: Luya is an essential ingredient in Filipino cuisine and home remedies.
Ginger.detailed_description: Ginger (Zingiber officinale) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Relieves nausea, Aids digestion, Anti-inflammatory. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Gumamela.name: Gumamela
Gumamela.description: Gumamela is a shrub with large, showy flowers. The flowers and leaves have medicinal properties.
Gumamela.cultural_relevance: Gumamela flowers are used for tea and as a natural hair conditioner.
Gumamela.detailed_description: Gumamela (Hibiscus rosa-sinensis) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Lowers blood pressure, Cough remedy, Hair conditioner. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Ipil-ipil.name: Ipil-ipil
Ipil-ipil.description: Ipil-ipil is a fast-growing tree with compound leaves. The seeds and pods are used medicinally.
Ipil-ipil.cultural_relevance: Ipil-ipil seeds are used traditionally for deworming, though with caution.
Ipil-ipil.detailed_description: Ipil-ipil (Leucaena leucocephala) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Intestinal worm expeller, Protein source, Fodder. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Jackfruit.name: Jackfruit
Jackfruit.description: Jackfruit is a tree with large fruits and medicinal leaves. It is the largest tree-borne fruit.
Jackfruit.cultural_relevance: Langka is valued both as food and medicine in the Philippines.
Jackfruit.detailed_description: Jackfruit (Artocarpus heterophyllus) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Boosts immunity, Aids digestion, Rich in nutrients. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Kakawate.name: Kakawate
Kakawate.description: Kakawate is a tree with compound leaves and pink flowers. It is used as live fence and medicine.
Kakawate.cultural_relevance: Madre de cacao is used for skin problems and as natural insecticide.
Kakawate.detailed_description: Kakawate (Gliricidia sepium) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats skin diseases, Insecticide, Rodenticide. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Kamias.name: Kamias
Kamias.description: Kamias is a tree with sour, oblong fruits. The fruits and leaves are used for cooking and medicine.
Kamias.cultural_relevance: Kamias is used for its sour taste in cooking and for skin bleaching.
Kamias.detailed_description: Kamias (Averrhoa bilimbi) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats cough, Bleaching agent, Anti-diabetic. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Kamote.name: Kamote
Kamote.description: Kamote is a root crop with nutritious tubers and leaves. It is easy to grow and widely cultivated.
Kamote.cultural_relevance: Kamote is a staple food and valued for its nutritional benefits.
Kamote.detailed_description: Kamote (Ipomoea batatas) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Energy source, Rich in vitamins, Anti-diabetic potential. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Katakataka.name: Katakataka
Katakataka.description: Katakataka is a succulent plant with fleshy leaves. It can grow from leaf cuttings easily.
Katakataka.cultural_relevance: Katakataka is used for cough and as a poultice for wounds.
Katakataka.detailed_description: Katakataka (Kalanchoe pinnata) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats cough, Anti-inflammatory, Wound healing. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Lemon.name: Lemon
Lemon.description: Lemon is a citrus tree with sour yellow fruits. It is rich in Vitamin C and widely used.
Lemon.cultural_relevance: Lemon is used for its health benefits and in various home remedies.
Lemon.detailed_description: Lemon (Citrus limon) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Rich in Vitamin C, Aids digestion, Skin lightening. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Mango.name: Mango
Mango.description: Mango is a tree with sweet, juicy fruits. The leaves and bark are also medicinal.
Mango.cultural_relevance: Mango is the national fruit of the Philippines and widely loved.
Mango.detailed_description: Mango (Mangifera indica) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Rich in vitamins, Anti-diabetic leaves, Treats cough. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Mango Ginger.name: Mango Ginger
Mango Ginger.description: Mango Ginger is a rhizome plant with mango-like aroma. It is used in cooking and medicine.
Mango Ginger.cultural_relevance: Mango ginger is used in traditional medicine and as a spice.
Mango Ginger.detailed_description: Mango Ginger (Curcuma amada) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Anti-inflammatory, Aids digestion, Relieves pain. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Mangosteen.name: Mangosteen
Mangosteen.description: Mangosteen is a tree with dark purple fruits and white sweet flesh. The rind is very medicinal.
Mangosteen.cultural_relevance: Mangosteen is called the 'queen of fruits' for its health benefits.
Mangosteen.detailed_description: Mangosteen (Garcinia mangostana) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Rich in antioxidants, Anti-inflammatory, Boosts immunity. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Oregano.name: Oregano
Oregano.description: Oregano is a succulent herb with fleshy leaves and strong aroma. It is different from Mediterranean oregano.
Oregano.cultural_relevance: Oregano is a popular home remedy for cough and asthma in the Philippines.
Oregano.detailed_description: Oregano (Coleus amboinicus) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Relieves cough, Treats colds, Anti-asthmatic. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Pandan.name: Pandan
Pandan.description: Pandan is a plant with long, narrow leaves. It is widely used for its fragrant aroma in cooking.
Pandan.cultural_relevance: Pandan is essential in Filipino cooking for its aromatic flavor.
Pandan.detailed_description: Pandan (Pandanus amaryllifolius) is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Mild laxative, Lowers blood pressure, Anti-rheumatic. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Papaya.name: Papaya
Papaya.description: Papaya is a tree with large, palmately lobed leaves. The fruit and leaves are both medicinal.
Papaya.cultural_relevance: Papaya is widely consumed and used for digestive health.
Papaya.detailed_description: Papaya (Carica papaya) contains papain enzyme that aids digestion and helps break down proteins. The fruit and leaves are both medicinal and used traditionally for digestive health.

Tanglad.name: Tanglad
Tanglad.description: Tanglad is a tall perennial grass with long, slender leaves and a strong lemon scent. It grows in clumps up to 2 meters tall and is widely cultivated throughout the Philippines.
preparation_steps: Wash fresh stalks and leaves thoroughly
Boil 2-3 stalks in 3 cups water for 10 minutes
Tanglad.cultural_relevance: Tanglad is essential in Filipino cuisine, particularly in sinigang and various meat dishes. It is also widely used as a traditional remedy for fever and digestive problems in Calbayog and throughout Samar.
Tanglad.detailed_description: Tanglad or Lemongrass (Cymbopogon citratus) is widely used in Philippine traditional medicine for its calming, digestive, and fever-reducing properties. It contains citral and geraniol which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.

Atis.name: Atis
Atis.description: Atis is a small deciduous tree with oblong leaves that produces sweet, creamy fruits with a distinctive scaly green skin. It grows well in tropical climates and is common in backyards across Samar.
preparation_steps: Boil 1 cup leaves in 2 cups water for 15 minutes
Strain and drink warm, 2-3 times daily
Atis.cultural_relevance: Atis is a favorite backyard fruit tree in Calbayog and throughout the Visayas. The leaves have been used traditionally as a fever remedy for children, and the sweet fruit is enjoyed by all ages.
Atis.detailed_description: Atis (Annona squamosa) leaves are traditionally used in Philippine folk medicine for treating fever, diarrhea, and intestinal parasites. The fruit is rich in Vitamin C and dietary fiber.

Gabi.name: Gabi
Gabi.description: Gabi is a perennial herbaceous plant with large, heart-shaped leaves that can grow up to 1 meter long. The starchy corms and edible leaves are important food sources in the Philippines.
preparation_steps: For food: Peel corms, boil thoroughly to remove oxalates
For insect bites: Crush fresh leaves and apply to affected area
For tea: Boil clean leaves briefly, strain and drink
Gabi.cultural_relevance: Gabi is a staple root crop in rural Samar and Calbayog. The leaves (laing) are cooked with coconut milk in the famous Bicol dish, though this preparation is also popular in Samar. It represents resilience as it grows well in wet conditions.
Gabi.detailed_description: Gabi or Taro (Colocasia esculenta) is an important root crop in the Philippines. The corms must be cooked thoroughly to remove calcium oxalate crystals. The leaves are used externally for insect bites and internally as a vegetable.

Sampaguita.name: Sampaguita
Sampaguita.description: Sampaguita is a small shrub with fragrant white flowers that bloom year-round. It has glossy green leaves and is the national flower of the Philippines. It grows abundantly in warm, tropical climates like Calbayog.
preparation_steps: For tea: Steep fresh flowers in hot water for 5 minutes
For headache: Crush flowers and apply to temples
For eye wash: Boil flowers, cool and use as gentle eye rinse
For aroma: String flowers into garlands for natural air freshener
Sampaguita.cultural_relevance: Sampaguita is the national flower of the Philippines and holds deep cultural significance. In Calbayog, it is commonly used in religious offerings, worn as garlands (sampaguita necklaces), and grown in home gardens for its fragrance and beauty.
Sampaguita.detailed_description: Sampaguita (Jasminum sambac) is the Philippines' national flower. Its fragrant blooms are used in traditional medicine for headaches, relaxation, and as an aromatic remedy for stress and anxiety.

Lanzones.name: Lanzones
Lanzones.description: Lanzones is a medium-sized fruit tree that produces clusters of sweet-sour yellowish fruits with translucent flesh. It has pinnate leaves and thrives in tropical climates, particularly in Southern Leyte and nearby areas like Calbayog.
preparation_steps: For fever: Boil bark in water and drink as decoction
For mosquito repellent: Burn dried lanzones peels
For diarrhea: Boil fruit peels and drink liquid
Eat fresh fruit for vitamin C and antioxidants
Lanzones.cultural_relevance: Lanzones is celebrated in the Camiguin Lanzones Festival and is common in nearby regions including parts of Samar. In Calbayog, the fruit is enjoyed during harvest season and the tree provides shade in rural communities.
Lanzones.detailed_description: Lanzones (Lansium parasiticum) is a popular tropical fruit native to Southeast Asia. The bark has traditional uses for fever and malaria, while the fruit is rich in vitamins and antioxidants.

Balimbing.name: Balimbing
Balimbing.description: Balimbing is a small tropical tree with compound leaves that produces distinctive star-shaped yellow fruits when sliced. The fruits are crisp, juicy, and range from sour to sweet depending on variety.
preparation_steps: Eat fresh ripe fruit for vitamins
For cough: Boil unripe fruit and drink juice
For high blood pressure: Drink fresh juice regularly
Add to salads for digestive health
Balimbing.cultural_relevance: Balimbing trees are common in backyards throughout Calbayog and Samar. The sour variety is used in cooking (sinigang, paksiw) while the sweet variety is eaten fresh. The star-shaped slices make it a decorative garnish for drinks and dishes.
Balimbing.detailed_description: Balimbing or Star Fruit (Averrhoa carambola) is valued for its distinctive shape and health benefits. It contains oxalates and should be consumed in moderation by those with kidney issues. Rich in Vitamin C and antioxidants.

Kalabasa.name: Kalabasa
Kalabasa.description: Kalabasa is a vine plant with large, lobed leaves and yellow-orange flowers. It produces large, round to elongated fruits with orange flesh and numerous seeds. It thrives in tropical climates and is extensively grown in Calbayog.
preparation_steps: Cook flesh in various dishes (ginataan, soup)
Boil young leaves and flowers as vegetable
Roast seeds and eat as deworming remedy
Make soup with flowers for respiratory health
Kalabasa.cultural_relevance: Kalabasa is a staple vegetable in Calbayog cuisine, used in ginataang kalabasa, soups, and various traditional dishes. The flowers (bulaklak ng kalabasa) are highly prized as a vegetable and are believed to be good for the lungs.
Kalabasa.detailed_description: Kalabasa or Squash (Cucurbita maxima) is an important vegetable crop in the Philippines. The orange flesh is rich in beta-carotene and vitamins. The flowers, leaves, and seeds all have traditional medicinal and culinary uses.

Alugbati.name: Alugbati
Alugbati.description: Alugbati is a fast-growing, soft-stemmed climbing vine with thick, fleshy, heart-shaped leaves. It produces dark purple berries and thrives in hot, humid climates. It is commonly found in gardens throughout Calbayog.
preparation_steps: Add to sinigang or sauté with garlic
Use as substitute for spinach in any dish
Alugbati.cultural_relevance: Alugbati is a common backyard vegetable in Calbayog households. It grows easily without much care and provides nutritious greens year-round. The purple juice from the berries is sometimes used as a natural food coloring.
Alugbati.detailed_description: Alugbati (Basella alba) is a popular leafy vegetable in the Philippines, similar to spinach but more heat-tolerant. It is rich in iron, calcium, and vitamins A and C. The mucilaginous texture is similar to spinach when cooked.

Kangkong.name: Kangkong
Kangkong.description: Kangkong is a semi-aquatic tropical plant with hollow stems and long, lance-shaped leaves. It grows prolifically in water or moist soil and is one of the most popular leafy vegetables in the Philippines.
preparation_steps: Wash thoroughly (especially if harvested from water)
Sauté with garlic and soy sauce (adobong kangkong)
Add to sinigang as green vegetable
Kangkong.cultural_relevance: Kangkong is the 'poor man's vegetable' in the Philippines due to its affordability and easy growth. In Calbayog, it grows wild in rice paddies, streams, and wetlands. Adobong kangkong is a beloved dish throughout the region.
Kangkong.detailed_description: Kangkong (Ipomoea aquatica) is a popular leafy vegetable in Southeast Asia. It grows rapidly in aquatic or moist environments. Rich in iron, vitamins, and minerals. It is a staple green vegetable in Filipino cuisine.

Neem.name: Neem
Neem.description: Neem is a fast-growing tree with medicinal bark, leaves, and seeds. It has been used in traditional medicine for thousands of years.
Neem.cultural_relevance: Neem is revered in traditional medicine and is commonly found in Indian and Southeast Asian communities.
Neem.detailed_description: Neem (Azadirachta indica) is a trees plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats skin diseases, Antibacterial, Antifungal. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Acacia.name: Acacia
Acacia.description: Acacia is a fast-growing tree with yellow flowers and distinctive curved seed pods. Some species have medicinal bark.
Acacia.cultural_relevance: Acacia trees are common in the Philippines and used for various purposes including shade and timber.
Acacia.detailed_description: Acacia (Acacia auriculiformis) is a trees plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats diarrhea, Sore throat remedy, Antiseptic. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Mahogany.name: Mahogany

Mahogany.cultural_relevance: Mahogany is prized for its timber and widely planted throughout the tropics.
Mahogany.detailed_description: Mahogany (Swietenia macrophylla) is a trees plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats diarrhea, Fever reducer, Antimalarial properties. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Narra.name: Narra

Narra.cultural_relevance: Narra is the Philippines' national tree, known for its beautiful timber and medicinal red sap.
Narra.detailed_description: Narra (Pterocarpus indicus) is a trees plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Treats mouth sores, Sore throat remedy, Antiseptic wash. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Rosal.name: Rosal
Rosal.description: Rosal is an evergreen shrub with glossy dark green leaves and fragrant white flowers. It is widely cultivated for its beauty and fragrance.
Rosal.cultural_relevance: Rosal flowers are prized for their sweet fragrance and are often used in garlands and religious offerings.
Rosal.detailed_description: Rosal (Gardenia jasminoides) is a ornamental plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Calming sedative, Treats headaches, Reduces fever. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Champaca.detailed_description: Champaca (Magnolia champaca) is a ornamental plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. Bougainvillea.detailed_description: Bougainvillea (Bougainvillea spectabilis) is a ornamental plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. Pineapple.detailed_description: Pineapple (Ananas comosus) is a fruits plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. Pomegranate.detailed_description: Pomegranate (Punica granatum) is a fruits plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Rich in antioxidants, Good for heart health, Anti-inflammatory. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Blueberry.description: Blueberry is a shrub with small blue-purple berries that grow in clusters. The berries are rich in antioxidants.

Blueberry.detailed_description: Blueberry (Vaccinium corymbosum) is a fruits plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. It is known for its medicinal properties including Rich in antioxidants, Good for brain health, Supports heart health. This plant has been used by Filipino communities for generations and continues to be an important part of traditional healing practices.

Cabbage.name: Cabbage

medicinal_uses: Rich in Vitamin C and K
Cabbage.cultural_relevance: Cabbage is a staple vegetable used in cuisines worldwide, from salads to kimchi.
Cabbage.detailed_description: Cabbage (Brassica oleracea var. capitata) is a vegetables plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine. Okra.detailed_description: Okra (Abelmoschus esculentus) is a vegetables plant traditionally used in Philippine traditional medicine.
